jokes.text: Oh, my God, is he dead? I... I don't know what happened.
jokes.setup: Someone says there's something wrong with Pete
jokes.punchline: Liz immediately jumps to the most extreme conclusion - death
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz Lemon catastrophizing - perfectly character-specific and economical escalation

jokes.text: Why would I even say that?
jokes.setup: Kenneth says 'What could go wrong?'
jokes.punchline: Immediately realizes he's just tempted fate
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-comedy that acknowledges the trope while using it - economy and self-awareness make this work

jokes.text: Hey, whatever happened to TiVo? Remember, you used to fast-forward, it'd make that sound? Boop-boop, boop-boop. Yeah. Yeah, then it'd be all like, bung-bung. - Boop-boop, boop-boop. - Bung-bung, bung-bung. - Bung-bung, bung-bung. - Boop-boop, boop-boop. Boop-boop, boop-boop.
jokes.setup: Question about TiVo technology
jokes.punchline: Couple gets lost making increasingly elaborate sound effects
jokes.explanation: Pure 30 Rock absurdism - the escalating sound effects are ridiculous but oddly relatable

jokes.text: And he/she has his/her baseball glove and baby. And both sets of genitals!
jokes.setup: Description of gender-neutral doll Terry
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of dual genitalia
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - starts innocent then drops absurd anatomical detail casually

jokes.text: That was hard to track down. Today, almost all of them are in police evidence lockers.
jokes.setup: Difficulty finding the doll
jokes.punchline: Dark implication about why these toys are confiscated
jokes.explanation: Exceptionally dark and unexpected - the casual delivery makes the disturbing implication even funnier

jokes.text: German flag, please.
jokes.setup: Criss asks if she wants a smiley face pancake
jokes.punchline: Liz requests German flag design instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz Lemon - choosing something unnecessarily complicated over simple joy

jokes.text: Jordan's comments have angered gay-rights groups and are likely annoying his co-workers who thought they'd linger over breakfast this morning, perhaps with a new lover. Wow, that is some detailed reporting, curry.
jokes.setup: Standard news report about Tracy's comments
jokes.punchline: Weirdly specific and personal details about co-workers' breakfast plans
jokes.explanation: Absurd specificity in news reporting - the breakfast lover detail is wonderfully random

jokes.text: Oh, you mean my tote bag. Yes, I need a new one.
jokes.setup: Someone calls her an 'old bag from TGS'
jokes.punchline: Liz thinks they're literally talking about her bag
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection - Liz's obliviousness to the insult creates the humor

jokes.text: He's dating the charmin cub. I thought they were babies.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Snuggles bear being gay
jokes.punchline: Revelation that mascots are in relationships; Liz's innocent confusion
jokes.explanation: Absurd corporate mascot romance combined with Liz's innocent confusion

jokes.text: Him. Him. Her when she's drunk. I genuinely don't know. That one's a puzzler.
jokes.setup: Liz asks how many co-workers are gay
jokes.punchline: Tracy points out various people with increasingly specific qualifiers
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation building to 'puzzler' - Tracy's casual assessment is hilariously matter-of-fact

jokes.text: They make the Japanese look like the greeks.
jokes.setup: Gay community is most organized community
jokes.punchline: Comparison using national stereotypes in unexpected way
jokes.explanation: Clever structure playing with ethnic stereotypes about organization

jokes.text: Being gay is stupid. If you want to see a penis, take off your pants. If I got turned into a gay, I'd sit around all day and look at my own junk.
jokes.setup: Jack asks what Tracy said that was offensive
jokes.punchline: Tracy's incredibly stupid and literal understanding of homosexuality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic - completely missing the point in a characteristically stupid way

jokes.text: Do you think the people of Raleigh, North Carolina turned Clay Aiken gay? Why not? The Bronx turned me dyslexic.
jokes.setup: Liz challenges Tracy's 'turned gay' comment
jokes.punchline: Tracy's equally absurd counter-example about dyslexia
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Tracy logic - the Bronx/dyslexia connection is perfectly absurd and quotable

jokes.text: I already called Glad, Liz Lemon. Thank you for calling Glad, stronger trash bags with less plastic. This is Diane. How may I help you? Hey Diane, it's Tracy Jordan. Sorry about what I said. Well, okeydokey.
jokes.setup: Tracy says he called to apologize to 'Glad'
jokes.punchline: He called the trash bag company instead of GLAAD
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy mistake - the sound-alike confusion is believable for his character

jokes.text: Remember when I offended stubborn people? That took forever to sort out.
jokes.setup: Tracy complaining about having to apologize
jokes.punchline: Self-aware joke about why stubborn people would be hard to reconcile with
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logical irony - of course stubborn people would be impossible to apologize to

jokes.text: His name is 'Chris,' and I'm sorry, but for my own reasons... And 'Chris' is spelled? No 'h' and two 's's.
jokes.setup: Jack interrogating Liz about her boyfriend's name
jokes.punchline: The unusual spelling reveals Jack's worst fears
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for Jack's snobbery - the spelling immediately confirms his suspicions

jokes.text: Criss is trying to... You can stop right there. He's an entrepreneur. He's currently meeting with investors in the hopes of starting an organic gourmet hot dog truck.
jokes.setup: Liz defending Criss's career
jokes.punchline: The business plan is exactly as pretentious as Jack feared
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation revealing the hipster cliche - Jack's horror is completely justified

jokes.text: Good God! Where does this person live? Don't worry about it. How bad can it be? Jersey city? His parents' apartment? It's not a walk-up, is it? He's actually been living with me for the past month. Ah.
jokes.setup: Jack's mounting horror about Criss's living situation
jokes.punchline: Each guess gets worse, culminating in the worst option
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure - Jack's 'Ah' reaction beat is the perfect punchline

jokes.text: Where does this person live? Don't worry about it. How bad can it be? Jersey city? His parents' apartment? It's not a walk-up, is it?
jokes.setup: Jack asking about living situation
jokes.punchline: Each guess gets progressively worse/more specific
jokes.explanation: Great escalation structure showing Jack's snobby assumptions getting more specific and judgmental.

jokes.text: Good God!
jokes.setup: Learning about Criss's food truck plan
jokes.punchline: Jack's horrified reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack reaction. The delivery and character integration make a simple exclamation funny.

jokes.text: My jaw stopped popping. Listen.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining how she's more relaxed with Criss
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates by moving her jaw, presumably showing improvement
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz - finding the weirdest way to demonstrate relationship benefits

jokes.text: Jenny McCarthy died? But who could have been slowly poisoning her? Was she poisoned? I have no way of knowing, because I'm just hearing about it.
jokes.setup: Kenneth mentions bad news
jokes.punchline: Jenna immediately assumes celebrity death and creates elaborate conspiracy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna narcissism - immediately jumping to dramatic celebrity conspiracy

jokes.text: He also said that he thinks, quote, 'sexuality is a continuum,' and he is but a voyager on a vast ocean of pleasure.
jokes.setup: Kenneth reporting maintenance worker's response
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly poetic and sexual statement from building maintenance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition - flowery sexual metaphor from unlikely source

jokes.text: I just found out this morning, Teri Polo and Ving Rhames called me at home.
jokes.setup: Jenna announcing her B-list status
jokes.punchline: Bizarre claim about random celebrity calls
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna delusion - the random celebrity pairing is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Especially now that I'm officially a 'B-list' celebrity, thanks to America's kids got singing.
jokes.setup: Jenna talking about her importance
jokes.punchline: She's proud of being B-list, from a kids' show
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna delusion - being proud of B-list status and a kids' singing show. Classic character work.

jokes.text: who in return thinks of you as sort of an albino slave-monkey.
jokes.setup: Jenna asks Kenneth to do a favor as a friend
jokes.punchline: She reveals her dehumanizing view of him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna casual cruelty delivered as if it's a compliment. The specificity of 'albino slave-monkey' is brilliantly awful.

jokes.text: Kenneth, do this for me, someone I hope you consider a friend, and who in return thinks of you as sort of an albino slave-monkey. Aw, you think of me?
jokes.setup: Jenna asking Kenneth for a favor with fake friendship
jokes.punchline: Kenneth is touched by the horrible description
jokes.explanation: Perfect character interaction - Kenneth's innocent reaction to the insult is beautifully pathetic

jokes.text: I guess someone's never been locked in a dog crate and thrown overboard for displeasing the sheikh.
jokes.setup: Kenneth says they don't have a key
jokes.punchline: Jenna casually references traumatic experience as solution
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - casual mention of horrific past experience as practical advice

jokes.text: And now, I am headed home for a 'nooner,' which is what I call having pancakes for lunch.
jokes.setup: Pete announces he's going home for a 'nooner'
jokes.punchline: Innocent redefinition of sexual term
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pete innocence - redefining adult term for wholesome activity

jokes.text: I am nonplussed, and that is the correct usage.
jokes.setup: Tracy expressing anger about being called an idiot
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates intelligence by correctly using commonly misused word
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke - Tracy proving he's not an idiot while being pedantically correct

jokes.text: Wait. Why don't they do that?
jokes.setup: Liz mentions gay people and looking at junk
jokes.punchline: Tracy genuinely questions his own logic
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Tracy actually thinking about his earlier statement. The innocent confusion is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Is that when you cry on the floor in your office? Nope, I do that when I see myself in the mirror on my birthday.
jokes.setup: Liz asks about Pete's crisis mode
jokes.punchline: Pete casually mentions even more pathetic regular behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pete sadness - the birthday mirror crying is beautifully pathetic

jokes.text: Crisis mode is when I down a couple of sleeping pills with scotch, pass out in my secret napping place, and hope I dream about that latina security guard.
jokes.setup: Pete explaining his actual crisis mode
jokes.punchline: The escalation to dangerous substance mixing and inappropriate dreams
jokes.explanation: Dark Pete comedy escalating to concerning behavior. The specificity makes it funnier while being disturbing.

jokes.text: Crisis mode is when I down a couple of sleeping pills with scotch, pass out in my secret napping place, and hope I dream about that latina security guard. The one you always kiss on the mouth. That's normal in Guatemala.
jokes.setup: Pete explaining his crisis management strategy
jokes.punchline: Increasingly concerning details ending with cultural justification
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing Pete's disturbing coping mechanisms and inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: The one you always kiss on the mouth. That's normal in Guatemala.
jokes.setup: Liz commenting on Pete's behavior
jokes.punchline: Pete's matter-of-fact cultural explanation
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment revealing Pete's background while normalizing inappropriate behavior.

jokes.text: Picking a lock is like riding a bike. They're both skills you need to escape the Atlanta Falcons' equipment room.
jokes.setup: Common idiom about lock picking
jokes.punchline: Unexpected specific reference to sports team trauma
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - starts with cliche then reveals disturbing specificity

jokes.text: They're the same lights poultry farms use to keep the birds from pecking each other to death.
jokes.setup: Jenna describing her special pink lights
jokes.punchline: Reveals the lights serve a violent purpose in agriculture
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Jenna's vanity - using anti-violence lights for beauty purposes

jokes.text: Kenneth. I'm going to tell you what I told Phil Spector. 'It's gonna be okay, baby. We just have to get some trash bags and get back here before anyone's the wiser. Then we can keep recording my album.'
jokes.setup: Jenna trying to calm Kenneth about the mercury situation
jokes.punchline: Reveals she once helped Phil Spector cover up something dark
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - casually referencing helping infamous producer with cover-up

jokes.text: I'm going to tell you what I told Phil Spector. 'It's gonna be okay, baby. We just have to get some trash bags and get back here before anyone's the wiser. Then we can keep recording my album.'
jokes.setup: Jenna tries to calm Kenneth about the mercury
jokes.punchline: She quotes herself covering up evidence with Phil Spector
jokes.explanation: Exceptional dark comedy that implies Jenna helped Phil Spector cover up a crime. The casual delivery and specific dialogue make it brilliant.

jokes.text: Kenneth. I'm going to tell you what I told Phil Spector. 'It's gonna be okay, baby. We just have to get some trash bags and get back here before anyone's the wiser. Then we can keep recording my album.'
jokes.setup: Jenna trying to calm Kenneth about the Mercury situation
jokes.punchline: Her comparison to covering up something with notorious Phil Spector
jokes.explanation: Dark celebrity reference that implies Jenna was involved in covering up Spector's crimes. Brilliantly disturbing.

jokes.text: Is that an Armenian name? Is he a space Armenian? I don't know. The Kardashians are Armenian, they're into black guys, so there's something to it...
jokes.setup: Discussion about Lando Calrissian's name
jokes.punchline: Criss creates elaborate ethnic theory about Star Wars
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist reasoning but relies on stereotypes without much craft

jokes.text: Oh, better hold on to this one. He's getting a free muffin soon. Wow, I've never seen a sunglass hut credit card before.
jokes.setup: Jack examining Criss's belongings
jokes.punchline: Reveals increasingly low-class financial situation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Jack's snobbery, though the sunglass hut card is a bit obvious

jokes.text: Wow, I've never seen a sunglass hut credit card before.
jokes.setup: Jack continues examining Criss's wallet
jokes.punchline: The existence of a Sunglass Hut credit card
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about retail credit cards. Jack's amazement at the card's existence is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Hmm. this guy might suck
jokes.setup: Jack examining ukulele with Obama sticker
jokes.punchline: Understated conclusion after seeing hipster artifacts
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement from Jack - economical delivery of his judgment

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, if you watch those games, that thing definitely has a menstrual cycle. Right?
jokes.setup: Discussion about Phillie Phanatic being female
jokes.punchline: Criss seriously analyzing mascot's biological cycles
jokes.explanation: Absurd but committed analysis of mascot biology - ridiculous but believable for Criss

jokes.text: Frat guys, DJs, loud-mouthed old bitches, investment bankers, the tramp-stamped, parrot-heads, anti-vaccination crusaders, and people who won't shut up about scuba diving. It's a whole other world down there.
jokes.setup: Tracy listing his idiot coalition
jokes.punchline: Escalating list ending with scuba diving enthusiast doing their catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating list with perfect ending - the scuba diving line is spot-on

jokes.text: It's a whole other world down there.
jokes.setup: Reference to scuba diving people
jokes.punchline: Immediate demonstration of the annoying scuba talk
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback that proves the point about annoying scuba enthusiasts. Great economy.

jokes.text: That's right. I'm an idiot. Surprised?
jokes.setup: Denise Richards introduced as celebrity spokesperson
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission of her reputation
jokes.explanation: Basic celebrity self-deprecation - works but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Well, I am. For all 'intensive purposes.'
jokes.setup: Denise claiming to be surprised
jokes.punchline: Uses malapropism while proving she's an idiot
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that proves the character's stupidity through common malapropism

jokes.text: Because as Braveheart said, 'you can take our freedom, unless you take our lives.'
jokes.setup: Tracy invoking famous movie quote
jokes.punchline: Completely mangles the famous line
jokes.explanation: Perfect idiot misremembering - the logic error in the quote is brilliantly wrong

jokes.text: I also have a lot of imaginary arguments with the couples on house hunters. Why can't people look past paint color?
jokes.setup: Liz admits Jack is in her head
jokes.punchline: She also argues with TV show participants
jokes.explanation: Good relatable observation about reality TV frustrations. Very character-specific neurotic behavior.

jokes.text: Although I don't know for sure, because I refuse to read literature that questions the morality of war.
jokes.setup: Jack mentions catch-22
jokes.punchline: Reveals he refuses to read anti-war literature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack conservative character moment - principled ignorance about literature

jokes.text: Although I don't know for sure, because I refuse to read literature that questions the morality of war.
jokes.setup: Jack mentions Catch-22
jokes.punchline: His principled refusal to read anti-war literature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack Donaghy moment showing his rigid conservative principles even in literary choices. Character integration is excellent.

jokes.text: Criss-a, Criss, Criss, Criss-a, Criss-a, Criss-a, Criss-a, Criss-a, Criss train!
jokes.setup: Liz defending Criss to Jack
jokes.punchline: Creates absurd chant that presumably involves dancing
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy moment but the chant goes on too long

jokes.text: I looked like Adriana Lima.
jokes.setup: Jack mentions Liz's blue contact lenses
jokes.punchline: Liz's delusional self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Good character delusion delivered with perfect confidence. The specific model reference makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Black nerds, jet blue passengers who fall asleep with the TV on, pets whose owners have died, and, uh, idiots.
jokes.setup: Jack asking who watches their show
jokes.punchline: Increasingly depressing demographic list
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark escalation about TV viewership - the dead pet owners detail is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: You wrote 'remember to DVR Kendra' on your hand.
jokes.setup: Jack says Liz can handle talking to idiots
jokes.punchline: Evidence of her own questionable viewing habits
jokes.explanation: Good character observation that reveals Liz's trashy TV habits. The hand-written reminder makes it more pathetic.

jokes.text: Pete! It's Jenna! The woman you're in love with!
jokes.setup: Jenna tries to wake unconscious Pete
jokes.punchline: Her narcissistic assumption about his feelings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna self-centeredness even in an emergency. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: 'Maroney found in closet with unconscious married man and inbred virgin.' Again? No way. I have too much to lose now.
jokes.setup: Jenna worried about scandal
jokes.punchline: Casual 'again' reveals this has happened before
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna reveal - the casual 'again' makes this even funnier

jokes.text: Vaginal mesh. Nice try, prolapse.
jokes.setup: Jenna mentioning vaginal mesh spokeswoman job
jokes.punchline: Creates advertising slogan for medical product
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd advertising slogan - perfectly captures Jenna's clueless approach

jokes.text: They'd kick me out of the page program faster than a fella can come up with folksy similes.
jokes.setup: Kenneth worried about getting fired
jokes.punchline: Self-aware joke about his own speech pattern
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor - Kenneth acknowledging his own folksy speech patterns

jokes.text: Dot com, hold the steering wheel. I've got to leave my outgoing message. What did I just hit? Was that a person? Is that paint or blood? Dot com, this did not happen. We take this to our graves!
jokes.setup: Tracy's voicemail message
jokes.punchline: Escalating panic about a possible hit-and-run
jokes.explanation: Great escalation in a voicemail message format. The panic and cover-up attempts are perfectly Tracy.

jokes.text: What did I just hit? Was that a person? Is that paint or blood? Dot com, this did not happen. We take this to our graves!
jokes.setup: Tracy leaving voicemail while driving
jokes.punchline: Escalating panic about apparent hit-and-run
jokes.explanation: Good dark escalation showing Tracy's panic and immediate cover-up instinct

jokes.text: I called sunglass hut and got my credit limit raised to $80, plus, they told me I only needed 5,000 more shades points to get free lens wipes
jokes.setup: Criss sharing good news with Liz
jokes.punchline: Pathetically small financial achievement
jokes.explanation: Good callback that builds on earlier setup - the low credit limit is perfectly pathetic

jokes.text: # Who's that man behind the curtain # His kiss will make you certain # of the one name you'll be blurtin' # during love-making # Kelsey, Kelsey #
jokes.setup: Mysterious romantic song begins
jokes.punchline: Reveals Jack is secretly seducing Criss through elaborate musical number
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd reveal - Jack's elaborate seduction scheme through musical performance

jokes.text: # his name is Kelsey, he's very wealthy # he doesn't have to be doing this #
jokes.setup: Continuing the seduction song
jokes.punchline: Breaks fourth wall about Jack's unnecessary involvement
jokes.explanation: Great meta-commentary within the absurd musical number - acknowledges Jack's privilege
